raw_consumer_statement:
This supplemental submission is provided to preserve the record and to document materially inconsistent and procedurally defective communications issued by American Express during the pendency of a CFPB-tracked complaint. 

As reflected in the attached exhibits, the consumer filed a CFPB complaint on XXXX XXXX XXXX reiterating that the disputed transactions remain unauthorized, that no admissions of authorization or benefit had been made, and that the consumer requested any investigative questions or information requests be provided in writing to ensure accuracy and avoid misunderstanding. 

On XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX the consumer made multiple outbound attempts to contact the assigned American Express fraud investigator, including calls lasting several minutes, and left voicemail messages requesting follow-up. These call attempts are documented in the attached phone records. No return call was received, and no investigative questions were ever provided in writing, despite repeated requests. The consumer also communicated with other representatives within the same department, who indicated that messages would be relayed to the assigned investigator. 

Despite this documented cooperation, on XXXX XXXX XXXX, the consumer received multiple emails from American Express stating that the fraud inquiries had been resolved or closed, while simultaneously asserting that American Express had been unable to reach the consumer by telephone and threatening reversal of previously issued credits absent further telephone discussion. These communications further referenced unspecified merchant documents, without providing those materials or any written investigative questions to the consumer.

These representations are procedurally irreconcilable. American Express can not credibly assert that an investigation has been completed and resolved while also claiming the consumer was unreachable and conditioning the preservation of credits on future contact. The attached exhibits demonstrate that the consumer actively attempted to engage, that calls were returned, and that American Express failed to follow through with written investigative requests. 

This submission does not withdraw any dispute, does not consent to reversal of credits, and does not authorize post-deadline reinvestigation. It is submitted solely to preserve the record and to assist the CFPB in evaluating procedural integrity, investigation consistency, and the accuracy of representations made to the consumer during the pendency of a CFPB-tracked complaint.
